title: JPMorgan Downgrades Enovix Stock Rating on Smartphone Market Concerns
text: JPMorgan downgraded Enovix Corporation (NASDAQ: ENVX) from Neutral to Underweight, citing significant challenges in the smartphone battery market. The stock trades at $6.88 with a $1.46 billion market cap. Primary Concerns: Energy Density Disadvantage - JPMorgan warns that Enovix's energy density lead is narrowing faster than the market appreciates as competitors close the performance gap. Commercialization Delays - The analyst expects the volume ramp will continue to slip and disappoint despite Honor qualification progress. Profitability Challenges - The firm believes Enovix will struggle to achieve profitability targets, noting that current valuation embeds an overly optimistic commercialization timeline. Limited Alternative Markets - Opportunities in wearables, defense, and drones will not be large enough to offset challenges in the smartphone segment. Analyst Action: JPMorgan analyst Bill Peterson withdrew the firm's prior price target of $6.
